REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS OVERRULED, THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUFFICIENTLY STATED. DEMURRER TO THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE ASSERTED AS THE 3RD CLAIM IN THIS ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND ON THIS PETITION AND PETITIONER FAILED TO ALLEGE "EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES AS TO THE EXACT ISSUES SUBJECT TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION" AS ORDERED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: KAREN A. AUFDERMAUR, CSR 10919 (T: 209.324.9532, E: KAUFDERMAUR@YAHOO.COM) (501/CFH)

ORDER ON STIPULATION RE: SUPPLEMENTAL STATEMENT AND JOINT REQUEST TO STRIKE ALL DECLARATIONS FROM RICHARD HANNUM AND RELATED BRIEFING

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION AND HRG ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OFF CALENDAR. THIS MATTER IS NOT READY FOR DETERMINATION BASED ON THE PARTIES' PERSISTENT SUPPLEMENTAL FILINGS WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT WELL BEYOND MOVING PAPERS, OPPOSITION AND REPLY. WHILE THESE FILINGS HAVE BEEN EVENTUALLY STRICKEN PURSUANT TO STIPULATION (2 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE CONTINUED HEARING) AT LEAST ONE OF THESE FILINGS WAS MADE IN SUPPORT OF AN AUTHORIZED FILING (REPLY BRIEF FILED ON SEPTEMBER 15, 2023); MOVING PARTY DID NOT REQUEST A LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED REPLY REMOVING THE NOW POTENTIALLY IRRELEVANT PARTS OR ANY DECLARATION ADDRESSING WHETHER THE REMOVAL OF THE DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE REPLY CHANGED THE REPLY ITSELF, LEAVING IT FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE WHICH PARTS OF THE REPLY ARE TO BE CONSIDERED AND WHICH ARE NOT IN LIGHT OF THE DECLARATION BEING STRICKEN. WHEN PARTIES ARE READY TO PRESENT THEIR FINAL ARGUMENTS FOR CONSIDERATION (NOT AN EVER-CHANGING MOVING TARGET) THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE MAY BE SET FOR HEARING. THE BRIEFS ON SUCH PETITION WILL BE LIMITED TO MOVING PAPERS, OPPOSITION AND REPLY. THERE WILL BE NO FILING PAST THE REPLY DEADLINE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONERS' MOTION AND HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 7, 2023 FOR THE PETITIONER TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF ADDRESSING THE PROCEDURAL PROPRIETY OF SEPTEMBER 29, 2023 FILING MADE 8 DAYS AFTER THE PREVIOUSLY SET HEARING DATE AND WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT. THE COURT NOTES THAT A RESPONSE TO THIS ADDITIONAL FILING WAS FILED ON OCTOBER 24, 2023, ONLY 2 DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING DATE. ADDITIONALLY, ALL PARTIES MUST FILE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS (NOT TO EXCEED 3 PAGES) ADDRESSING THE SUBSTANTIVE AND PROCEDURAL CHANGES TO THE POSTURE OF THIS PETITION IN LIGHT OF THE DECLARATION OF RICHARD HANNUM FILED ON OCTOBER 25, 2023, 1 DAY BEFORE THE HEARING. THE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS MUST BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 1, 2023. NEITHER PARTY IS AUTHORIZED TO MAKE ANY FILINGS IN SUPPORT OF OR IN OPPOSITION TO THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE OTHER THAN THE FILINGS DESCRIBED HEREIN PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON NOVEMBER 7, 2023. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO, CSR #5229, PEGGYTSUJIMOTO@GMAIL.COM, 415-828-1919 (D501)

calendar.matters:
Amended Notice Of Motion And Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Ryan J. Patterson and Brian O'Neill; Patterson & O'Neill, PC; 415-907-9110; appearing as counsel for petitioners and plaintiffs Yes in my Back Yard and Sonja Trauss.
Kristen A. Jensen and Audrey W. Pearson; 415-554-4709; appearing as counsel for respondent and defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Petitioners contest the court's tentative decision. Counsel argue as to whether or not the controversy in this action has been mooted. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PETITIONER YES IN MY BACK YARD, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT, SONJA TRAUSS AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE, is taken under submission.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Brianna Rudd, CSR #13668, briannarudd@gmail.com, 209-747-1306 (D501)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-12-2024 Continued To Feb-23-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Ryan Patterson & Brian O'Neill of Patterson & O'Neill, PC 415-907-9110 appearing for petitioner.
Kristen Ann Jensen, Kathy Shin & Audrey Pearson of the San Francisco Deputy City Attorneys, 415-554-4615 appearing for respondent.
Reporter: Brianna Rudd; CSR# 13668; 209-747-1306; Briannarudd@gmail.com

Motion And Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PETITIONER YES IN MY BACK YARD, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT, SONJA TRAUSS Motion And Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is OFF CALENDAR. This matter is not ready for determination based on the parties' persistent supplemental filings without leave of Court well beyond moving papers, opposition and reply. While these filings have been eventually stricken pursuant to stipulation (2 court days before the continued hearing) at least one of these filings was made in support of an authorized filing (reply brief filed on September 15, 2023); moving party did not request a leave to file an amended reply removing the now potentially irrelevant parts or any declaration addressing whether the removal of the declaration in support of the reply changed the reply itself, leaving it for the Court to determine which parts of the reply are to be considered and which are not in light of the declaration being stricken.
When parties are ready to present their final arguments for consideration (not an ever-changing moving target) the petition for writ of mandate may be set for hearing. The briefs on such petition will be limited to moving papers, opposition and reply. There will be no filing past the reply deadline.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion And Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-26-2023 Continued To Nov-07-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Ryan J. Patterson and Brian J. O'Neill; Patterson & O'Neill, PC; 415-907-9110; appearing as counsel for Petitionrs and Plaintiffs Yes In My Backyard and Sonia Trauss.
Kathy J. Shin and Audrey W. Pearson; Deputy City Attorneys; 415-554-4700; appearing as counsel for Respondent and Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Petitioners contest the court's tentative ruling. Respondent has a conflict with the tentative date of continuance. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PETITIONERS' MOTION AND HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is continued to November 7, 2023 for the petitioner to file a supplemental brief addressing the procedural propriety of September 29, 2023 filing made 8 days after the previously set hearing date and without leave of Court. The Court notes that a response to this additional filing was filed on October 24, 2023, only 2 days before the hearing date. Additionally, all parties must file supplemental briefs (not to exceed 3 pages) addressing the substantive and procedural changes to the posture of this Petition in light of the Declaration of Richard Hannum filed on October 25, 2023, 1 day before the hearing. The supplemental briefs must be filed and served no later than November 1, 2023. Neither party is authorized to make any filings in support of or in opposition to the Petition for Writ of Mandate other than the filings described herein prior to the hearing on November 7, 2023.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, peggytsujimoto@gmail.com, 415-828-1919 (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE

Appearances: Kathy Shin and Audrey Pearson of the City Attorney's Office, (415.554.47009) for respondent and moving party; Ryan Patterson and Brian O'Neill of Zacks Freedman & Patterson, PC (415.956.8100) for petitioner and opposing party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is OVERRULED, this cause of action is sufficiently stated. Demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate asserted as the 3rd claim in this action is sustained without leave to amend. The Court previously granted leave to amend on this petition and Petitioner failed to allege "exhaustion of administrative remedies as to the exact issues subject to this cause of action" as ordered. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Karen A. Aufdermaur, CSR 10919 (T: 209.324.9532, E: kaufdermaur@yahoo.com) (501/CFH)
